xanesnet.losses package¶

Public API for XANESNET loss functions.

class xanesnet.losses.Loss(loss_type)[source]¶

Bases: Module, ABC

Abstract base class for all XANESNET loss functions.

Parameters:

loss_type (str) – Identifier string for the concrete loss type.

__init__(loss_type)[source]¶

Initialize Loss.

abstractmethod forward(preds, targets)[source]¶

Compute the scalar loss between predictions and targets.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Model output predictions (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth target values (B, N).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Loss tensor.

class xanesnet.losses.LossRegistry[source]¶

Bases: object

Name-based registry for loss classes.

classmethod register(name)[source]¶

Register a loss class under name.

Parameters:

name (str) – Registry key. Matching is case-insensitive.

Return type:

Callable[[type[Loss]], type[Loss]]

Returns:

Decorator that registers and returns the class unchanged.

Raises:

KeyError – If name is already registered.

classmethod get(name)[source]¶

Return the loss class registered as name.

Parameters:

name (str) – Registry key. Matching is case-insensitive.

Return type:

type[Loss]

Returns:

Registered loss class.

Raises:

KeyError – If no loss is registered under name.

classmethod list()[source]¶

Return all registered loss names.

Return type:

list[str]

Returns:

Registry keys in insertion order.

class xanesnet.losses.BCEWithLogitsLoss(loss_type)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

Binary cross-entropy loss with logits.

Parameters:

loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

__init__(loss_type)[source]¶

Initialize BCEWithLogitsLoss.

forward(preds, targets)[source]¶

Compute binary cross-entropy loss with logits.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Raw (unnormalized) model logits (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Binary target labels in [0, 1] with shape (B, N).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Scalar loss tensor.

class xanesnet.losses.EMDLoss(loss_type)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

Earth Mover’s (Wasserstein) distance loss.

Computes the discrete 1-D Earth Mover’s Distance on a unit-spaced spectral grid as the L1 distance between the cumulative spectra of preds and targets.

Parameters:

loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

__init__(loss_type)[source]¶

Initialize EMDLoss.

forward(preds, targets)[source]¶

Compute the Earth Mover’s Distance loss.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Model output predictions (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth spectral targets (B, N).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Scalar loss tensor summed over spectral bins and averaged over the batch dimension.

class xanesnet.losses.L1Loss(loss_type)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

L1 (mean absolute error) loss.

Parameters:

loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

__init__(loss_type)[source]¶

Initialize L1Loss.

forward(preds, targets)[source]¶

Compute the L1 (mean absolute error) loss.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Model output predictions (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth target values (B, N).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Scalar loss tensor.

class xanesnet.losses.MSELoss(loss_type)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

Mean squared error loss.

Parameters:

loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

__init__(loss_type)[source]¶

Initialize MSELoss.

forward(preds, targets)[source]¶

Compute the mean squared error loss.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Model output predictions (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth target values (B, N).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Scalar loss tensor.

class xanesnet.losses.MultiKernel_SSIM_1D(loss_type, N, fractions=(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25), data_range=1.0, K=(0.01, 0.03), device='cpu', use_weighted_sum=False, weights=None, final_combine=True, final_mean=True)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

Multi-kernel SSIM loss for 1-D signals.

Evaluates SSIM at several Gaussian kernel sizes, where each scale is determined as a fraction of the signal length N. Scales can be combined multiplicatively (default) or via a weighted sum.

Parameters:

  • loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

  • N (int) – Length of the input signal.

  • fractions (list[float] | tuple[float, ...]) – Kernel size fractions of N for each SSIM scale. Defaults to [0.01, 0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25].

  • data_range (float) – Dynamic range of the signal (max - min). Defaults to 1.0.

  • K (tuple[float, float]) – Stability constants (K1, K2) for luminance and contrast-structure terms. Defaults to (0.01, 0.03).

  • device (str | device) – Compatibility argument retained for configuration parity. Kernels are moved to the device of preds during forward. Defaults to 'cpu'.

  • use_weighted_sum (bool) – If True, combine scales via weighted sum instead of the default multiplicative combination. Defaults to False.

  • weights (list[float] | None) – Per-scale weights when use_weighted_sum=True. If None, uniform weights are used. Defaults to None.

  • final_combine (bool) – If True, collapse scales into a single map. If False, return a per-scale stack. Defaults to True.

  • final_mean (bool) – If True, reduce the loss map to one value per batch element. Defaults to True.

__init__(loss_type, N, fractions=(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25), data_range=1.0, K=(0.01, 0.03), device='cpu', use_weighted_sum=False, weights=None, final_combine=True, final_mean=True)[source]¶

Initialize MultiKernel_SSIM_1D.

forward(preds, targets, mask=None)[source]¶

Compute the multi-kernel 1-D SSIM loss.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Predicted signals (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth signals (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor | None) – Optional mask tensor with shape (B, N) or broadcastable to the unreduced loss map. Defaults to None.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Scalar loss tensor when final_mean=True. Otherwise returns the unreduced loss map with shape (B, N) when final_combine=True or (B, num_scales, N) when final_combine=False.

class xanesnet.losses.SpectralLossPlus(loss_type, blur_sigma_bins=5.0, alpha=0.4, beta=0.6, gamma=0.2, huber_delta=0.01, kappa_peak=0.15)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

Multi-component spectral loss combining blurred, detail, and gradient terms.

The total loss is alpha * Lc + beta * Ld + gamma * Lg where:

  • Lc - coarse MSE between Gaussian-blurred preds and targets.

  • Ld - peak-aware weighted MSE on the residual fine detail.

  • Lg - Huber loss on first-order gradients (shape consistency).

Parameters:

  • loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

  • blur_sigma_bins (float) – Gaussian blur width in spectral bins for the coarse component. Defaults to 5.0.

  • alpha (float) – Weight for the coarse loss Lc. Defaults to 0.4.

  • beta (float) – Weight for the detail loss Ld. Defaults to 0.6.

  • gamma (float) – Weight for the gradient loss Lg. Defaults to 0.2.

  • huber_delta (float) – Transition point for the Huber loss. Defaults to 0.01.

  • kappa_peak (float) – Amplitude of the peak-aware weight boost applied to Ld. Defaults to 0.15.

__init__(loss_type, blur_sigma_bins=5.0, alpha=0.4, beta=0.6, gamma=0.2, huber_delta=0.01, kappa_peak=0.15)[source]¶

Initialize SpectralLossPlus.

forward(preds, targets, mask=None)[source]¶

Compute the spectral loss.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Model output predictions (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth spectral targets (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor | None) – Unused; reserved for future use. Defaults to None.

Returns:

alpha * Lc + beta * Ld + gamma * Lg.

Return type:

Tensor

gaussian_blur1d(y, sigma_bins, k=None)[source]¶

Blur a batch of 1-D signals with a Gaussian kernel.

Parameters:

  • y (Tensor) – Input signals (B, N).

  • sigma_bins (float | Tensor) – Gaussian standard deviation in spectral bins. If a 1-D tensor, only its maximum value is used.

  • k (int | None) – Kernel length. If None, derived from sigma_bins as ceil(6 * sigma) rounded up to the nearest odd number.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Blurred signals with the same shape as y.

Raises:

ValueError – If k is not a positive odd integer.

huber_loss(x, y, delta=0.01, reduction='mean')[source]¶

Compute the Huber (smooth L1) loss between two tensors.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – First input tensor.

  • y (Tensor) – Second input tensor (reference).

  • delta (float) – Transition point between quadratic and linear regions. Defaults to 0.01.

  • reduction (str) – Reduction mode - 'mean', 'sum', or 'none'. Defaults to 'mean'.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Loss tensor (scalar for 'mean'/'sum', element-wise for 'none').

Raises:

ValueError – If reduction is not 'mean', 'sum', or 'none'.

peak_weighting(y, kappa=0.15)[source]¶

Compute a peak-aware spatial weight map.

Upweights strong intensities and concave regions to direct the detail loss towards spectrally important features.

Parameters:

  • y (Tensor) – Input signals (B, N).

  • kappa (float) – Amplitude of the weight boost applied at peaks and concavities. Defaults to 0.15.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Weight map (B, N) with values >= 1, detached from the computation graph.

class xanesnet.losses.WCCLoss(loss_type, gaussian_hwhm=10)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

Weighted cross-correlation (WCC) loss.

Computes the WCC similarity between predictions and targets using a Gaussian weight function centred at zero lag, following the method of Kallman et al. [1].

References

[1] Kallman, E., Delcey, M.G., Guo, M., Lindh, R. and Lundberg, M.

(2020). Quantifying similarity for spectra with a large number of overlapping transitions: Examples from soft X-ray spectroscopy. Chemical Physics, 535, p.110786.

Parameters:

  • loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

  • gaussian_hwhm (int) – Half-width at half-maximum of the Gaussian weight function in spectral bins. Defaults to 10.

__init__(loss_type, gaussian_hwhm=10)[source]¶

Initialize WCCLoss.

forward(preds, targets)[source]¶

Compute the WCC loss.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Model output predictions (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth spectral targets (B, N).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Scalar loss tensor in [0, 1].

Submodules¶

xanesnet.losses.base module¶

Abstract base class for all XANESNET loss functions.

class xanesnet.losses.base.Loss(loss_type)[source]¶

Bases: Module, ABC

Abstract base class for all XANESNET loss functions.

Parameters:

loss_type (str) – Identifier string for the concrete loss type.

__init__(loss_type)[source]¶

Initialize Loss.

abstractmethod forward(preds, targets)[source]¶

Compute the scalar loss between predictions and targets.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Model output predictions (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth target values (B, N).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Loss tensor.

  • targets (Tensor) – Binary target labels in [0, 1] with shape (B, N).

Tensor

Scalar loss tensor.

Earth Mover’s Distance (Wasserstein) loss for XANESNET.

class xanesnet.losses.emd.EMDLoss(loss_type)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

Earth Mover’s (Wasserstein) distance loss.

Computes the discrete 1-D Earth Mover’s Distance on a unit-spaced spectral grid as the L1 distance between the cumulative spectra of preds and targets.

Parameters:

loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

Initialize EMDLoss.

forward(preds, targets)[source]¶

Compute the Earth Mover’s Distance loss.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Model output predictions (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth spectral targets (B, N).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Scalar loss tensor summed over spectral bins and averaged over the batch dimension.

xanesnet.losses.mkssim1d module¶

Multi-kernel SSIM loss for 1-D signals.

class xanesnet.losses.mkssim1d.MultiKernel_SSIM_1D(loss_type, N, fractions=(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25), data_range=1.0, K=(0.01, 0.03), device='cpu', use_weighted_sum=False, weights=None, final_combine=True, final_mean=True)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

Multi-kernel SSIM loss for 1-D signals.

Evaluates SSIM at several Gaussian kernel sizes, where each scale is determined as a fraction of the signal length N. Scales can be combined multiplicatively (default) or via a weighted sum.

Parameters:

  • loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

  • N (int) – Length of the input signal.

  • fractions (list[float] | tuple[float, ...]) – Kernel size fractions of N for each SSIM scale. Defaults to [0.01, 0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25].

  • data_range (float) – Dynamic range of the signal (max - min). Defaults to 1.0.

  • K (tuple[float, float]) – Stability constants (K1, K2) for luminance and contrast-structure terms. Defaults to (0.01, 0.03).

  • device (str | device) – Compatibility argument retained for configuration parity. Kernels are moved to the device of preds during forward. Defaults to 'cpu'.

  • use_weighted_sum (bool) – If True, combine scales via weighted sum instead of the default multiplicative combination. Defaults to False.

  • weights (list[float] | None) – Per-scale weights when use_weighted_sum=True. If None, uniform weights are used. Defaults to None.

  • final_combine (bool) – If True, collapse scales into a single map. If False, return a per-scale stack. Defaults to True.

  • final_mean (bool) – If True, reduce the loss map to one value per batch element. Defaults to True.

__init__(loss_type, N, fractions=(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25), data_range=1.0, K=(0.01, 0.03), device='cpu', use_weighted_sum=False, weights=None, final_combine=True, final_mean=True)[source]¶

Initialize MultiKernel_SSIM_1D.

forward(preds, targets, mask=None)[source]¶

Compute the multi-kernel 1-D SSIM loss.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Predicted signals (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth signals (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor | None) – Optional mask tensor with shape (B, N) or broadcastable to the unreduced loss map. Defaults to None.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Scalar loss tensor when final_mean=True. Otherwise returns the unreduced loss map with shape (B, N) when final_combine=True or (B, num_scales, N) when final_combine=False.

xanesnet.losses.registry module¶

Class-level registry for XANESNET loss functions.

class xanesnet.losses.registry.LossRegistry[source]¶

Bases: object

Name-based registry for loss classes.

classmethod register(name)[source]¶

Register a loss class under name.

Parameters:

name (str) – Registry key. Matching is case-insensitive.

Return type:

Callable[[type[Loss]], type[Loss]]

Returns:

Decorator that registers and returns the class unchanged.

Raises:

KeyError – If name is already registered.

classmethod get(name)[source]¶

Return the loss class registered as name.

Parameters:

name (str) – Registry key. Matching is case-insensitive.

Return type:

type[Loss]

Returns:

Registered loss class.

Raises:

KeyError – If no loss is registered under name.

classmethod list()[source]¶

Return all registered loss names.

Return type:

list[str]

Returns:

Registry keys in insertion order.

xanesnet.losses.specplus module¶

Spectral loss with blurred coarse, detail, and gradient components.

class xanesnet.losses.specplus.SpectralLossPlus(loss_type, blur_sigma_bins=5.0, alpha=0.4, beta=0.6, gamma=0.2, huber_delta=0.01, kappa_peak=0.15)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

Multi-component spectral loss combining blurred, detail, and gradient terms.

The total loss is alpha * Lc + beta * Ld + gamma * Lg where:

  • Lc - coarse MSE between Gaussian-blurred preds and targets.

  • Ld - peak-aware weighted MSE on the residual fine detail.

  • Lg - Huber loss on first-order gradients (shape consistency).

Parameters:

  • loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

  • blur_sigma_bins (float) – Gaussian blur width in spectral bins for the coarse component. Defaults to 5.0.

  • alpha (float) – Weight for the coarse loss Lc. Defaults to 0.4.

  • beta (float) – Weight for the detail loss Ld. Defaults to 0.6.

  • gamma (float) – Weight for the gradient loss Lg. Defaults to 0.2.

  • huber_delta (float) – Transition point for the Huber loss. Defaults to 0.01.

  • kappa_peak (float) – Amplitude of the peak-aware weight boost applied to Ld. Defaults to 0.15.

__init__(loss_type, blur_sigma_bins=5.0, alpha=0.4, beta=0.6, gamma=0.2, huber_delta=0.01, kappa_peak=0.15)[source]¶

Initialize SpectralLossPlus.

forward(preds, targets, mask=None)[source]¶

Compute the spectral loss.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Model output predictions (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth spectral targets (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor | None) – Unused; reserved for future use. Defaults to None.

Returns:

alpha * Lc + beta * Ld + gamma * Lg.

Return type:

Tensor

gaussian_blur1d(y, sigma_bins, k=None)[source]¶

Blur a batch of 1-D signals with a Gaussian kernel.

Parameters:

  • y (Tensor) – Input signals (B, N).

  • sigma_bins (float | Tensor) – Gaussian standard deviation in spectral bins. If a 1-D tensor, only its maximum value is used.

  • k (int | None) – Kernel length. If None, derived from sigma_bins as ceil(6 * sigma) rounded up to the nearest odd number.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Blurred signals with the same shape as y.

Raises:

ValueError – If k is not a positive odd integer.

huber_loss(x, y, delta=0.01, reduction='mean')[source]¶

Compute the Huber (smooth L1) loss between two tensors.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – First input tensor.

  • y (Tensor) – Second input tensor (reference).

  • delta (float) – Transition point between quadratic and linear regions. Defaults to 0.01.

  • reduction (str) – Reduction mode - 'mean', 'sum', or 'none'. Defaults to 'mean'.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Loss tensor (scalar for 'mean'/'sum', element-wise for 'none').

Raises:

ValueError – If reduction is not 'mean', 'sum', or 'none'.

peak_weighting(y, kappa=0.15)[source]¶

Compute a peak-aware spatial weight map.

Upweights strong intensities and concave regions to direct the detail loss towards spectrally important features.

Parameters:

  • y (Tensor) – Input signals (B, N).

  • kappa (float) – Amplitude of the weight boost applied at peaks and concavities. Defaults to 0.15.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Weight map (B, N) with values >= 1, detached from the computation graph.

xanesnet.losses.wcc module¶

Weighted cross-correlation (WCC) loss for XANESNET.

class xanesnet.losses.wcc.WCCLoss(loss_type, gaussian_hwhm=10)[source]¶

Bases: Loss

Weighted cross-correlation (WCC) loss.

Computes the WCC similarity between predictions and targets using a Gaussian weight function centred at zero lag, following the method of Kallman et al. [1].

References

[1] Kallman, E., Delcey, M.G., Guo, M., Lindh, R. and Lundberg, M.

(2020). Quantifying similarity for spectra with a large number of overlapping transitions: Examples from soft X-ray spectroscopy. Chemical Physics, 535, p.110786.

Parameters:

  • loss_type (str) – Identifier string for this loss type.

  • gaussian_hwhm (int) – Half-width at half-maximum of the Gaussian weight function in spectral bins. Defaults to 10.

__init__(loss_type, gaussian_hwhm=10)[source]¶

Initialize WCCLoss.

forward(preds, targets)[source]¶

Compute the WCC loss.

Parameters:

  • preds (Tensor) – Model output predictions (B, N).

  • targets (Tensor) – Ground-truth spectral targets (B, N).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Scalar loss tensor in [0, 1].
